title: Data analysts
description: Analyze and coordinate the ongoing logistical functions of a firm or organization. Responsible for the entire life cycle of a product, including acquisition, distribution, internal allocation, delivery, and final disposal of resources. Design or analyze operational solutions for projects such as transportation optimization, network modeling, process and methods analysis, cost containment, capacity enhancement, routing and shipment optimization, or information management. Analyze product delivery or supply chain processes to identify or recommend changes. May manage route activity including invoicing, electronic bills, and shipment tracing. (Based on 21 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Review, classify, and record survey data in preparation for computer analysis.
Prepare statistical data for reports to regulatory agencies, managers, or clients.
Analyze statistical information to estimate mortality, accident, sickness, disability, and retirement rates.
Examine theories such as probability and inference to improve methods of obtaining and evaluating numerical data.
Design research projects using valid scientific techniques and historical data for analyses.
Plan data collection methods for projects and specify sample groups to be used.
Evaluate sources of information to assess limitations in reliability and usability.
Analyze and interpret statistical data to identify significant relationships and trends.
Report statistical analysis results, including graphs, charts, and tables.
Conduct surveys and collect data using various methods like interviews and questionnaires.
Collaborate with researchers and stakeholders to identify problems and recommend solutions.
Define data requirements and validate information using judgment and statistical tests.
Analyze data from surveys or old records using statistical software.
Develop mathematical or statistical models for analysis or computational simulation.
Design and validate databases for storing and processing biological or clinical data.
Document database changes and develop procedures for data management.
Investigate business intelligence data and synthesize findings to support recommendations for action.
Manage the flow of business intelligence information to relevant users.
Communicate with stakeholders to stay updated on industry or business trends.
Identify or monitor current and potential customers, using business intelligence tools.
Maintain library of model documents, templates, or other reusable knowledge assets, and document specifications for reports and dashboards.
Provide technical support for existing reports, dashboards, or other tools and maintain or update business intelligence tools and systems.
Develop technical specifications for data management programming and communicate needs to information technology staff.
Compile reports, charts, or graphs that describe and interpret findings of analyses, and prepare tables, graphs, fact sheets, and written reports summarizing research results.
Check source data to verify completeness and accuracy, and verify the accuracy and validity of data entered in databases, correcting any errors.
Compute and analyze data using statistical formulas and computers or calculators, perform descriptive and multivariate statistical analyses, and assess trends in trade literature.
Conduct research on consumer opinions and marketing strategies, collaborating with marketing professionals and other experts.
Collect and analyze data on customer demographics, preferences, needs, and buying habits to identify potential markets and factors affecting product demand.
Develop and implement technical project management tools such as plans, schedules, and matrices, and provide logistical facility or capacity planning analyses.
Propose logistics solutions for customers and evaluate logistic opportunities for cost-reduction or process improvement.
Provide project management services, including the provision and analysis of technical data.
Participate in the assessment and review of design alternatives and design change proposal impacts.
Support the development of training materials and technical manuals and train workers in new procedures or equipment.
Stay informed of logistics technology advances and apply appropriate technology to improve logistics processes.
Redesign the movement of goods to maximize value, minimize costs, and enhance supply-chain efficiency.
Manage the logistical aspects of product life cycles, including coordination of samples and minimization of obsolescence.
Develop logistic metrics, analysis tools, or key performance indicators for business units and evaluate logistics processes.
Create models or scenarios to predict the impact of variables like fuel costs or carbon emissions legislation on logistics.
Analyze logistics data, using methods like data mining, data modeling, or cost/benefit analysis, and interpret logistics data.
Prepare reports on logistics performance measures and monitor industry standards to identify trends in logistics planning.
Manage systems to ensure pricing structures reflect logistics costing accurately and monitor inventory transactions.
Confer with logistics teams to determine optimization strategies for service levels and cost reduction opportunities.
Identify opportunities for inventory reductions and determine logistics support requirements, including facility needs.
Develop or maintain models for logistics uses, such as cost estimating or demand forecasting, and maintain logistics records.
Document findings and prepare recommendations for implementing new systems, procedures, or changes.
Gather and organize information on logistics problems or procedures and recommend improvements to existing processes.
Maintain databases of logistics information and enter logistics-related data into databases.
Review proposals and contractual commitments to determine logistics requirements and analyze support systems.
Review logistics records and forms for efficiency, compliance, and identification of problems or improvements.
